analyst_priority: MEDIUM downgraded to LOW (no new actionable findings; pure pattern-continuation)

kernel_of_truth.summary: The dome's auto-pipeline is doing the right thing in a narrow, mechanical sense: it is computing daily status (12 status_history.json appends, eclipse_days countdown, the auto-update commits keep running) and not adjudicating things it cannot mechanically adjudicate. There is genuine engineering discipline visible in build_daily_review.py keying only off explicit FALSIFIED labels — that's defensible isolation between mechanical computation and judgment.
kernel_of_truth.where_the_kernel_fails: The discipline is being asymmetrically applied. The author has a refined bucket available and is not using it on closed-window predictions. status_history.json continues to claim wins_confirmed=67 (against site-displayed 69) — internal counter mismatch unchanged. The pipeline being alive enough to compute status but not alive enough to adjudicate IS the operational signature of 'don't book the loss' even if the local engineering choice (don't auto-flip on missing labels) is principled. Our verdict on PRED-105 (tacit_withdrawal) was advanced specifically because the principle 'pipeline alive, adjudication frozen' is itself the falsification.

Defense: source-file SHAs are byte-identical across 6+ polls, so refactor is not the explanation. Could also be that the auto-pipeline computed nothing-new and the registry counts genuinely shouldn't move; this is harder to rule out — but the 2026-04-30 EOM closures created 8 events that any non-broken adjudication pipeline should have at least proposed status changes for.
